Title: Manifest-driven discovery
Summary: How Bleepforge discovers and lists .tres files for any manifest-declared domain. manifestCache singleton, projectIndex extension with classifiers for all 4 entry kinds, the /api/manifest-domain endpoint, dynamic sidebar nav, and the read-only MVP routes at /manifest. Shipped in v0.2.7.
Body:
## The shape

When your project's `bleepforge_manifest.json` declares a domain, Bleepforge surfaces it like a built-in domain:

1. The sidebar grows a **Manifest** section listing every declared domain.
2. Clicking a domain opens `/manifest/<domain>` — an identity-only table of every `.tres` Bleepforge discovered for it.
3. Ctrl+K finds the domain by name, with a teal **Domain** badge to distinguish it from FoB's hardcoded set.

This is **read-only** in v0.2.7. The discovery half of the pipeline is complete; the write half (generic edit UI + writeTresGeneric dispatch) lands in v0.2.9, and the JSON-cache half (generic importer) lands in v0.2.8.

FoB-only projects (empty manifest) see no visual change — the Manifest section is hidden entirely.

## Two singletons feed the discovery

**manifestCache** ([server/src/lib/manifest/cache.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/server/src/lib/manifest/cache.ts)) — holds the parsed manifest in memory. Built at boot from the same loader the `/api/manifest` diagnostics endpoint uses; refreshed when the file watcher detects a change to `bleepforge_manifest.json` (the user re-exports from godot-lib's Tools menu, or a CI build hook fires, or a hand edit lands). O(1) lookups by domain name + by sub-resource name.

**projectIndex** (the runtime singleton that maps every authored Godot resource to its `.tres` file) was extended to consume manifestCache during its content-classifying walk. The walk runs once at boot + incrementally on every watcher event; each `.tres` matches against either a FoB hardcoded classifier OR a manifest-derived classifier.

## Classification rules per entry kind

The classifier in [server/src/lib/projectIndex/build.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/server/src/lib/projectIndex/build.ts) handles all four:

- **`domain`** — match `script_class === entry.class`, extract the key field's value. Key field type drives the regex shape: string-quoted for `string`/`multiline`/`flag`, bare-int for `int`/`enum`. Identity = the extracted value.
- **`foldered`** — match `script_class === entry.class` + parent-dir convention (`parentDir` or `grandparentDir`) + optional `parentNameMustBe` defense check. Identity = `<folder>/<basename>` composite.
- **`enumKeyed`** — match `script_class === entry.class`. Read the key field's int value; identity = `entry.enumValues[intValue]`. Default-omission handled: Godot drops the line when value === enum's first variant, so missing key → `enumValues[0]`.
- **`discriminatedFamily`** — match `script_class` against the base class OR any variant class. Identity = base's key field value. The variant class is preserved on the index entry's `scriptClass` field for downstream consumers.

## Classifier order matters: manifest first

**Manifest classifiers run BEFORE FoB's hardcoded ones.** This is the load-bearing direction of the genericization arc — the user's manifest is the source of truth for their project; FoB's hardcoded set is a fallback that retires when FoB ports.

The order was discovered the hard way during test-project validation. FoB's classifier for items uses the heuristic "any resource with a `Slug` field, regardless of script_class" — to catch every `ItemData` subclass without a stale allowlist. But that's greedy: test-project's `Note` and `Equipment` resources also have Slug fields and were getting eaten as items before reaching the manifest path. Flipping the order to manifest-first fixed it. Empty/missing manifest produces a no-op loop, so FoB itself stays unaffected.

## HTTP surface

Two endpoints in [server/src/lib/manifest/domainRouter.ts](https://github.com/ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge/blob/main/server/src/lib/manifest/domainRouter.ts):

- `GET /api/manifest-domain` — index of every declared domain (name, kind, class, view, overrideUi, entityCount). Cheap; the client fetches on every nav.
- `GET /api/manifest-domain/:domain` — the declared manifest Entry + the IndexedTres entities. 404 when the domain isn't declared.

Identity-only payload — each entity row carries `id` + `absPath` + `resPath` + `scriptClass` + `folder`. **No field values extracted from the `.tres`** — that's the generic importer's job in v0.2.8. v0.2.7 proves discovery works; rendering rich rows comes next.

## Client surface

- `/manifest` — landing page (card grid of declared domains, empty-state copy pointing at godot-lib install when no domains exist).
- `/manifest/:domain` — discovered-entity table for one domain. Header shows manifest metadata (kind, class, view, overrideUi); body adapts to the kind (discriminatedFamily adds a `variant` column, foldered adds a `folder` column).
- **Sidebar** — `useManifestDomains()` hook fetches the index endpoint and renders a dynamic section below the FoB nav. Hidden when no domains. Each row shows `displayName ?? domain` with a tooltip carrying kind + entityCount.
- **AppSearch** — `buildSearchItems` indexes each declared domain as a `kind: "manifest"` row with a teal **Domain** badge. Entity-level indexing waits for v0.2.9 (when entities have routable edit pages — today an entity match would link to the same domain list page as a domain match).

## Reaction to manifest changes

The godot-lib emitter fires on three triggers (editor load, Tools menu, build hook). When any of those re-writes `bleepforge_manifest.json`, Bleepforge's watcher picks up the change → `manifestCache.refresh()` → cache updates → projectIndex's classifiers see the new domain shape on the next walk.

For the catalog refresh on the client side, `useManifestDomains` subscribes to the existing `catalog-bus` so Ctrl+K + the sidebar + the index page all re-fetch when something invalidates the catalog. No new SSE channel — the catalog bus already covers the rare-but-real "manifest changed during this session" case.

- [Generic .tres mapper](/help/under-the-hood/generic-tres-mapper) — the write half of the generic pipeline.
